title: See through the screen

placement.where: replaces the torch-and-material walkthrough plus Activity 6.6 sorter; students shine light through each material to reveal what they can see, then sort every object into Transparent / Translucent / Opaque (Table 6.4)

windowMethods: test(itemIdOrArray) — SHORTCUT: places item AND shines the light in one call to reveal transparency; id is 'glass_tumbler'|'window_glass'|'butter_paper'|'frosted_glass'|'eraser'|'wooden_board' or array.

studentControls.control: Pick a material chip (glass tumbler, window glass, butter paper, frosted glass, eraser, wooden board)
studentControls.effect: places that material on the screen in front of the friend and clears any previous reveal
reveals what you can see through the placed material — clear (transparent), fuzzy outline (translucent), or fully blocked (opaque) — and marks it explored

studentControls.control: Tap an object tile, then tap a see-through bin
studentControls.effect: if the object belongs in that family it locks in green with a one-line reason, score and streak rise; otherwise a gentle hint shows, wrongTaps increases, and the bin shakes (no lock)
clears the screen, all sorts, wrong tries and streak, and restocks the tray

transparent.label: Transparent
transparent.meaning: You can see clearly straight through it; almost all light passes.

translucent.label: Translucent
translucent.meaning: Some light passes but the view is fuzzy; you see only a blurry outline.

opaque.label: Opaque
opaque.meaning: No light passes through; the view is completely blocked.

items.name: Glass tumbler
items.reason: Clear glass — you can see straight through it.

items.name: Window glass
items.reason: A clear window — you see the outside view clearly.

items.name: Butter paper
items.reason: Light passes through, but the view is blurry — not clear.

items.name: Frosted glass
items.reason: Blurry — light passes but no clear view.

items.name: Eraser
items.reason: No light passes through — completely blocks the view.

items.name: Wooden board
items.reason: Wood blocks all light — you cannot see through it.

answerKey.rule: Classify a material by how much light passes through it: transparent (see clearly), translucent (fuzzy view), opaque (no view).
answerKey.operatorModeNote: ai = focused demo: the see-through screen and read-out stay live, the material picker / shine button / sorting taps are hidden or inert and the agent drives via select()/reveal()/test()/sort()/highlight(); student = full interactive controls. Default student.

scientificFacts: Transparent materials such as clear glass let almost all light pass through, so you can see objects behind them clearly.
Translucent materials such as butter paper or frosted glass let some light through but scatter it, so you see only a blurry outline.
Opaque materials such as wood or an eraser block light completely, so you cannot see through them at all.
The same material can be sorted by a simple test: shine light or place it in front of an object and check how clearly you can see.
